---
title: "Musicology_Project"
author: "Job van de Visse"
date: "2026-02-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r load packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
Chester<- read.csv("Chester.csv")
summary(Chester)

Emily<- read.csv("Emily.csv")
summary(Emily)
```

```{r}
Chester |>
  summarise(
    mean_speechiness = mean(Speechiness),
    mean_acousticness = mean(Acousticness),
    mean_liveness = mean(Liveness),
    sd_speechiness = sd(Speechiness),
    sd_acousticness = sd(Acousticness),
    sd_liveness = sd(Liveness),
    median_speechiness = median(Speechiness),
    median_acousticness = median(Acousticness),
    median_liveness = median(Liveness),
    mad_speechiness = mad(Speechiness),
    mad_acousticness = mad(Acousticness),
    mad_liveness = mad(Liveness)
  )
```

```{r}

# Combine datasets and add era identifier
Chester <- Chester %>% mutate(Era = "Chester Bennington")
Emily <- Emily %>% mutate(Era = "Emily Armstrong")

combined_data <- bind_rows(Chester, Emily)

# Select key audio features and reshape for plotting
features_to_compare <- c("Energy", "Valence", "Danceability", 
                         "Acousticness", "Speechiness", "Loudness")

plot_data <- combined_data %>%
  select(Era, all_of(features_to_compare)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Era, names_to = "Feature", values_to = "Value")

# Normalize Loudness to 0-1 scale for fair comparison
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(Value = ifelse(Feature == "Loudness", 
                        (Value + 60) / 60,  # Spotify loudness typically -60 to 0
                        Value))

# Create the visualization
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Feature, y = Value, fill = Era)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = 21, outlier.size = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Chester Bennington" = "#D32F2F", 
                               "Emily Armstrong" = "#1976D2"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Linkin Park's Sonic Evolution: Chester Bennington vs Emily Armstrong",
       subtitle = "Comparing audio features across lead-singer transitions",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Feature Value (0-1 scale)",
       caption = "Data: Spotify API | Loudness normalized to 0-1 scale") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0, color = "gray30"),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "left",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5)
  )
```


```{r}
# For exploring relationships between features
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = Energy, y = Valence, color = Era)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Chester Bennington" = "#D32F2F", 
                                "Emily Armstrong" = "#1976D2"),
                     name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Energy vs. Emotional Tone: A Tale of Two Eras",
       subtitle = "Does the new lineup maintain Linkin Park's signature intensity?",
       x = "Energy Level",
       y = "Valence (Musical Positivity)",
       caption = "Data: Spotify API") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.position = c(0.85, 0.15),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```


```{r}
# Faceted view of all major features
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Era, y = Value, fill = Era)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6, draw_quantiles = c(0.5)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Feature, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Chester Bennington" = "#D32F2F", 
                               "Emily Armstrong" = "#1976D2")) +
  labs(title = "Linkin Park's Musical DNA: Before and After",
       subtitle = "Track-level analysis reveals continuity and change",
       x = NULL,
       y = "Feature Value",
       caption = "Data: Spotify API") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r Breakout evening 2}
library(compmus)

```

```{r Breakout evening 2.1}

wood <- read_csv("wood.csv")


```

```{r}
wood |>
  compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
  mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "euclidean")) |>
  compmus_gather_chroma() |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = start + duration / 2,
      width = duration,
      y = pitch_class,
      fill = value
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = NULL, fill = "Magnitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
```

```{r Numb}
ChromaNumb <- read_csv("ChromaNumb.csv")

ChromaNumb|>
  compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
  mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "chebyshev")) |>
  compmus_gather_chroma() |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = start + duration / 2,
      width = duration,
      y = pitch_class,
      fill = value
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = NULL, fill = "Magnitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c()
```

```{r Ave Maria}

## The Tallis Scholars
tallis <- read_csv("tallis.csv")
## La Chapelle Royale
chapelle <- read_csv("chapelle.csv")
## Oxford Camerata
oxford <- read_csv("oxford.csv")
## Chanticleer
chanticleer <- read_csv("chanticleer.csv")
## The Hilliard Ensemble
hilliard <- read_csv("hilliard.csv")
## The Gabrieli Consort
gabrieli <- read_csv("gabrieli.csv")

compmus_long_distance(
  hilliard |> 
    compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
    mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "chebyshev")) |> 
    filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L),
  oxford |> 
    compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
    mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "chebyshev")) |> 
    filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L),
  feature = pitches,
  method = "euclidean"
) |>
  filter(!is.nan(d)) |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = xstart + xduration / 2,
      width = 50 * xduration,
      y = ystart + yduration / 2,
      height = 50 * yduration,
      fill = d
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(x = "Hilliard", y = "Oxford") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(guide = NULL)
```

```{r Normal vs a capella}


Chromaempti <- read_csv("ChromaEmpti.csv")

Chromaemptiacapella <- read_csv("ChromaemptiAcapella.csv")


Chromaempti|>
  compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
  mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |>
  compmus_gather_chroma() |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = start + duration / 2,
      width = duration,
      y = pitch_class,
      fill = value
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = NULL, fill = "Magnitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c()

Chromaemptiacapella|>
  compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
  mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |>
  compmus_gather_chroma() |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = start + duration / 2,
      width = duration,
      y = pitch_class,
      fill = value
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(x = "Time (s)", y = NULL, fill = "Magnitude") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c()






compmus_long_distance(
  Chromaempti |> 
    compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
    mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |> 
    filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L),
  Chromaemptiacapella |> 
    compmus_wrangle_chroma() |> 
    mutate(pitches = map(pitches, compmus_normalise, "manhattan")) |> 
    filter(row_number() %% 50L == 0L),
  feature = pitches,
  method = "manhattan"
) |>
  filter(!is.nan(d)) |> 
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = xstart + xduration / 2,
      width = 50 * xduration,
      y = ystart + yduration / 2,
      height = 50 * yduration,
      fill = d
    )
  ) +
  geom_tile() +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(x = "Normal", y = "Acapella") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(guide = NULL)
```



```{r}
library(gitcreds)
```
